schema_status: canonical — tenant manifest required before activation

metadata.title: Response to Active CVE Exploitation — CVE-2009-0238 / CVE-2026-32201

AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS.description: Hostnames of endpoints in incident scope

AFFECTED_ACCOUNTS.description: UPNs or SAM names of compromised accounts

MALICIOUS_HASHES.description: SHA-256 hashes of confirmed malicious files

MALICIOUS_IPS.description: IPs confirmed malicious in this incident

SUSPICIOUS_URLS.description: URLs flagged for sandbox or TI review

RELEASE_TIMESTAMP.description: Set at R-001 execution — used by R-002 monitoring window

INTERNAL_IP_RANGES.description: Internal network CIDR ranges — used in Q-002 to exclude non-external connections

SOL_TTL_DAYS.description: Suspicious Object Management entry TTL in days

MONITORING_WINDOW_HOURS.description: Post-release monitoring window in hours for R-002 and R-003

APPROVAL_IR_LEAD.description: Contact identity for APR-001 approval — IR Lead

APPROVAL_IR_MANAGER.description: Escalation contact when APR-001 approval times out

APPROVAL_TIMEOUT_MINUTES.description: Minutes before APR-001 timeout escalation triggers

PATCH_TEAM_CONTACT.description: Contact for EH-001 patch deployment handoff

PATCH_TEAM_SLA_HOURS.description: SLA hours for patch deployment confirmation

FIREWALL_TEAM_CONTACT.description: Contact for EH-002 perimeter firewall handoff

FIREWALL_TEAM_SLA_HOURS.description: SLA hours for perimeter firewall block confirmation

BACKUP_TEAM_CONTACT.description: Contact for EH-003 backup restore handoff (conditional)

IDENTITY_TEAM_CONTACT.description: Contact for EH-004 account reset fallback (activates when FEATURE_IDENTITY_SECURITY=false)

POST_INCIDENT_REPORT_OWNER.description: Contact for EH-005 post-incident report distribution

FEATURE_IDENTITY_SECURITY.description: Identity Security integration available — enables RA-007 at C-004

FEATURE_ASRM.fallback_if_false: skip T-001 and PI-002, document gap
FEATURE_ASRM.description: Attack Surface Risk Management licensed and populated

FEATURE_NETWORK_SENSOR.fallback_if_false: Q-002 uses endpoint-reported telemetry only — coverage may be incomplete
FEATURE_NETWORK_SENSOR.description: Network sensor present in tenant for Q-002

FEATURE_DETECTION_MODEL_MGMT.fallback_if_false: skip D-002 and PI-001, document gap
FEATURE_DETECTION_MODEL_MGMT.description: Detection Model Management edit access available

SANDBOX_QUOTA_REMAINING.description: Available Threat Analysis Center submission quota — check before T-004

triggers.condition: technique_id IN [T1203, T1068] AND severity IN [high, critical]
triggers.note: Analyst manually confirms trigger before activating playbook

triggers.condition: category IN [Exploitation, Privilege Escalation] AND severity IN [high, critical]
Analyst confirms suspected or active CVE exploitation

steps.title: Review Workbench for T1203 and T1068 correlated incidents
steps.objective: Identify auto-correlated incident alerts and establish initial affected endpoint scope
tenant_dependencies: Workbench enabled in tenant
analyst_tasks: Filter open incidents by technique IDs T1203 and T1068
Review Impact Scope panel and extract affected endpoint list into AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS
Record Workbench incident ID and correlated event count
Note incident timeline start timestamp — use as TIME_WINDOW_START if not already set
expected_outputs: Workbench incident record
AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS populated from Impact Scope
escalation_criteria: OAT confirmed on more than one endpoint in Impact Scope
Privilege escalation technique present in Workbench correlation
steps.notes: If no relevant Workbench incidents found, proceed to D-002 to check Detection Model Management directly before concluding absence of signal

steps.title: Review OAT alerts for Exploitation and Privilege Escalation
steps.objective: Confirm technique-level detection signals for affected endpoints
preconditions: D-001 completed
tenant_dependencies: FEATURE_DETECTION_MODEL_MGMT = true
analyst_tasks: Filter OAT alerts by categories Exploitation and Privilege Escalation
Scope filter to endpoints in AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS
Record alert severity, technique ID, and matched endpoint name per alert
expected_outputs: OAT alert list with severity, technique ID, and matched endpoint per alert
escalation_criteria: OAT severity = critical on any endpoint
Chained OAT alert immediately followed by network connection event on same endpoint
steps.notes: If FEATURE_DETECTION_MODEL_MGMT = false: skip to D-003 and document detection coverage gap

steps.title: Search for exploit child process chain
steps.objective: Identify suspicious process chains from vulnerable application parent processes consistent with CVE-2009-0238
preconditions: Q-001 field names validated against tenant telemetry schema
tenant_dependencies: Q-001 field names validated in tenant — see Query Catalog Q-001
Endpoint sensor telemetry ingested for TIME_WINDOW_START through TIME_WINDOW_END
analyst_tasks: Substitute AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS and time window into Q-001 and execute
Review results for unexpected child processes spawned from vulnerable application
Extract dropped file paths for C-005 collection targeting
Extract file hashes and add confirmed malicious items to MALICIOUS_HASHES
Correlate result timestamps with D-002 OAT alert timestamps
expected_outputs: Process execution records with parent-child chain
Dropped file paths identified
MALICIOUS_HASHES updated with recovered payload hashes
escalation_criteria: Unexpected child process spawned from vulnerable application confirmed
Dropped file written to Temp, AppData, or ProgramData path
steps.notes: Adapt Q-001 parent process filter to match the vulnerable application for CVE-2026-32201 if it differs from Excel. See Query Catalog Q-001 for full adaptation guidance

steps.title: Search for post-exploit outbound network connections
steps.objective: Identify external C2 or exfiltration connections from affected endpoints following exploitation
preconditions: Q-002 field names validated against tenant telemetry schema
INTERNAL_IP_RANGES populated in tenant config
tenant_dependencies: Q-002 field names validated — see Query Catalog Q-002
FEATURE_NETWORK_SENSOR or endpoint-reported network telemetry available for affected endpoints
analyst_tasks: Substitute AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS, INTERNAL_IP_RANGES, and time window into Q-002 and execute
Correlate connection timestamps with D-003 process events — flag connections within 5 minutes of exploit event
Add confirmed external malicious IPs to MALICIOUS_IPS
Add confirmed malicious domains or URLs to MALICIOUS_DOMAINS and SUSPICIOUS_URLS
expected_outputs: Outbound connection records with destination IP, port, and timestamp
MALICIOUS_IPS updated from confirmed external destinations
escalation_criteria: External connection confirmed within 5 minutes of D-003 exploitation event
Destination IP matches known malicious infrastructure per Threat Intelligence
steps.notes: If FEATURE_NETWORK_SENSOR = false: query depends on endpoint-reported network events only. Coverage may be incomplete for server-class endpoints. Document telemetry gap if no network events appear for a known-active endpoint

steps.title: Review Risk Index for affected endpoints
steps.objective: Prioritize response sequence by asset risk level and business sensitivity
preconditions: D-001 completed
FEATURE_ASRM = true
tenant_dependencies: FEATURE_ASRM = true
Asset data populated in Attack Surface Risk Management for affected endpoints
analyst_tasks: Review Risk Index score per endpoint in AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS using Executive Dashboard
Cross-reference against CMDB or asset inventory to confirm data classification and privilege level
Determine if any endpoint is a domain controller, database server, or hosts privileged service accounts
Produce prioritized response sequence: highest risk first
expected_outputs: Risk score per endpoint
Prioritized endpoint response sequence
escalation_criteria: Risk Index = critical on any affected endpoint
Affected endpoint is a domain controller, database server, or privileged account host
steps.notes: If FEATURE_ASRM = false: analyst manually prioritizes endpoints based on known asset sensitivity. Document that Risk Index data was unavailable

steps.title: Search for lateral movement from compromised endpoint
steps.objective: Determine if exploitation has spread beyond the initial host via remote execution tooling
preconditions: T-001 completed
Primary compromised endpoint identified in AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS
tenant_dependencies: Q-003 field names validated — see Query Catalog Q-003
Endpoint sensor process telemetry available
analyst_tasks: Substitute primary endpoint hostname and time window into Q-003 and execute
Review results for PsExec, WMI, net use, WMIC, and schtasks command patterns
If lateral movement confirmed: add target hostnames to AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS and return to T-001 for expanded Risk Index review
If expanded scope confirmed: repeat T-003 for new endpoints before proceeding to T-004
expected_outputs: Lateral movement attempt records or zero-result confirmation
AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS updated if lateral movement to additional hosts confirmed
escalation_criteria: Lateral movement to any second endpoint confirmed
Domain controller identified as lateral movement target
steps.notes: Q-003 field name objectCmd may be commandLine or processCmd in some tenants — validate before use. See Query Catalog Q-003

steps.title: Search for LSASS process access on affected endpoints
steps.objective: Detect credential dumping activity via LSASS memory access on compromised hosts
preconditions: T-001 completed
tenant_dependencies: TELEMETRY_PROCESS_ACCESS event type available in tenant — process access auditing enabled at sensor level
Q-004 field names validated — see Query Catalog Q-004
analyst_tasks: Substitute AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS and time window into Q-004 and execute
For any LSASS access results: confirm accessing process is not a known security tool or system process
Build and document per-tenant exclusion list for known legitimate LSASS-accessing processes
expected_outputs: LSASS access event records with accessing process identity
Zero-result confirmation if no access detected
escalation_criteria: LSASS access from non-system, non-security process confirmed on any endpoint
steps.notes: If TELEMETRY_PROCESS_ACCESS events are absent from tenant: flag as telemetry gap. This step cannot confirm or deny LSASS access without this event type. Document the gap before proceeding

steps.title: Submit payload to Threat Analysis Center for behavioral detonation
steps.objective: Obtain behavioral analysis of recovered payload to confirm C2, persistence, and secondary stage capabilities
required_inputs: Suspicious file artifact from D-003 paths or C-005 collection
preconditions: FEATURE_SANDBOX = true
SANDBOX_QUOTA_REMAINING > 0
File artifact available — not already in Threat Intelligence with known verdict
tenant_dependencies: FEATURE_SANDBOX = true
Threat Analysis Center submission quota confirmed
analyst_tasks: Submit suspicious file or URL to Threat Analysis Center
Review detonation report for: persistence mechanisms, C2 network indicators, dropped secondary payloads, anti-analysis behavior
Extract network IOCs from report and add to MALICIOUS_IPS and MALICIOUS_DOMAINS
Extract hashes of dropped payloads and add to MALICIOUS_HASHES
expected_outputs: Detonation report with behavioral summary
IOC list extracted from detonation report
C2 and persistence confirmation status
escalation_criteria: Detonation report confirms active C2 capability
Detonation report confirms persistence mechanism
Detonation report identifies additional payload stage
steps.blocking_condition: FEATURE_SANDBOX = true AND file artifact available
steps.notes: If FEATURE_SANDBOX = false or quota exhausted: skip to T-005 and document that behavioral analysis was not performed. Do not submit known-clean or confirmed-malicious (already in Threat Intelligence) files to conserve quota

steps.title: Threat Intelligence IOC reputation lookup
steps.objective: Validate confirmed IOC classification and finalize IOC lists for Suspicious Object Management submission
preconditions: At least one IOC available from D-003, D-004, or T-004
tenant_dependencies: Threat Intelligence portal accessible in tenant
analyst_tasks: Query Threat Intelligence portal for reputation of each IOC in MALICIOUS_HASHES, MALICIOUS_IPS, and MALICIOUS_DOMAINS
Note threat classification, confidence level, and attribution data per IOC
Remove tentative IOCs that return clean or unknown verdict — update variable lists accordingly
Finalize confirmed IOC lists as input to C-002
expected_outputs: Reputation result per IOC
Finalized confirmed malicious IOC lists for C-002
Attribution context if available from Threat Intelligence
escalation_criteria: IOC attributed to known threat actor or active campaign in current Threat Intelligence

steps.title: Isolate affected endpoints
steps.objective: Prevent further exploitation and lateral movement while preserving Vision One telemetry via management channel
preconditions: T-005 completed — triage confirmed
AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS finalized and prioritized
APR-001 approval from IR Lead received
Service impact assessment completed for critical endpoints
tenant_dependencies: Response Management write permission: Endpoint Isolation granted to operator
Active endpoint sensor on all AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS
Endpoint agents online and reachable by Vision One at execution time
analyst_tasks: Confirm isolation status = Isolated in Response Management console for each endpoint
Verify Vision One management channel remains active post-isolation for all isolated endpoints
Record isolation timestamp per endpoint
expected_outputs: Endpoint status: Isolated for all AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS
Management channel confirmed active post-isolation
escalation_criteria: Isolation fails on any endpoint — agent offline or unreachable
Endpoint shows continued outbound connections after isolation
More than 3 endpoints require simultaneous isolation — engage IR Lead for coordination
steps.notes: Endpoint isolation severs all user and service connectivity immediately. Confirm whether any endpoint is a domain controller, file server, or critical service host before executing — service impact is immediate. Isolation is reversible via R-001

steps.title: Block confirmed IOCs in Suspicious Object Management
steps.objective: Deploy estate-wide blocking for confirmed malicious files, IPs, domains, and URLs via sensor push
preconditions: T-005 completed — IOC lists finalized and confirmed
Suspicious Object Management write permission available to operator
tenant_dependencies: Suspicious Object Management write permission granted to operator
Sensor push to all relevant sensor groups enabled and propagating
SOL_TTL_DAYS set per organizational retention policy
analyst_tasks: After submission: validate deployment status per sensor group in Suspicious Object Management console
Confirm sensors acknowledged push — check deployment status field per entry
Record TTL expiry date per entry for R-004 SOL renewal check
expected_outputs: Suspicious Object Management entries created for all confirmed IOCs
Deployment status confirmed per sensor group
TTL expiry date recorded per entry
escalation_criteria: Further exploitation attempts detected on other endpoints after deployment
Deployment status shows pending indefinitely for any sensor group
steps.notes: Suspicious Object Management entries block matching IOCs estate-wide across all sensors — not only AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS. Verify each IOC is confirmed malicious before submitting. Entries expire at SOL_TTL_DAYS — set renewal reminder for R-004

steps.title: Terminate active malicious processes
steps.objective: Stop active malicious execution on isolated endpoints
required_inputs: Malicious process PID or image name from D-003 or T-002 results
C-001 isolation confirmed for target endpoints
preconditions: C-001 isolation confirmed for all target endpoints
Malicious process confirmed still running via XDR Search App before execution
IR Lead verbal authorization obtained and documented in incident record
tenant_dependencies: Response Management write permission: Process Termination granted to operator
Active endpoint agent on target endpoints
analyst_tasks: Confirm target process is still running via XDR Search App before executing termination
Execute termination by PID or image name in Response Management
Run follow-up XDR Search to confirm process no longer appears in telemetry
Document IR Lead verbal authorization in incident record with timestamp
expected_outputs: Process termination confirmed in Response Management audit log
Follow-up XDR Search confirming process absence
escalation_criteria: Process respawns after termination — active persistence mechanism not yet identified
steps.approval_required: IR Lead verbal authorization — documented, not a formal APR gate
steps.notes: Irreversible. Collect file artifacts via C-005 before terminating if memory forensics may be needed. Do not terminate system processes or security agent processes

steps.title: Reset compromised account credentials
steps.objective: Invalidate compromised account credentials and terminate active sessions
preconditions: AFFECTED_ACCOUNTS identified from T-002 or T-003 results
FEATURE_IDENTITY_SECURITY = true
IDENTITY_INTEGRATION_TYPE != none
APR-001 approval received
Service account impact assessment completed — service owners notified if applicable
tenant_dependencies: FEATURE_IDENTITY_SECURITY = true
IDENTITY_INTEGRATION_TYPE = azure_ad OR on_prem_ad OR hybrid
Identity Security integration status = Connected in Vision One
analyst_tasks: After reset: verify no re-authentication from AFFECTED_ACCOUNTS via XDR Search — query account field post-reset timestamp
For Azure AD accounts: confirm refresh token revocation in addition to password reset
Notify service owner if any reset account is confirmed as a service account
external_handoffs: EH-004 if FEATURE_IDENTITY_SECURITY = false or IDENTITY_INTEGRATION_TYPE = none
expected_outputs: Password reset confirmed per account
Active sessions terminated
Zero re-authentication from affected accounts confirmed post-reset
escalation_criteria: Account re-authenticates before reset completes
FEATURE_IDENTITY_SECURITY = false — route to EH-004 immediately
Service account reset causes dependent service failure
steps.blocking_condition: APR-001 approval received AND FEATURE_IDENTITY_SECURITY = true (else route to EH-004)
steps.notes: If FEATURE_IDENTITY_SECURITY = false or IDENTITY_INTEGRATION_TYPE = none: this step becomes external. Activate EH-004 immediately — compromised credentials remain exploitable until reset is confirmed

steps.title: Collect file artifacts from affected endpoints
steps.objective: Preserve forensic evidence before eradication — required precondition for E-001
required_inputs: Malicious file paths from D-003 results
C-001 isolation confirmed for target endpoints
preconditions: C-001 isolation confirmed
File paths identified from D-003 results
File collection quota available in Response Management
tenant_dependencies: Response Management write permission: File Collection granted to operator
File collection feature available in this tenant license tier
Active endpoint agent on target endpoints
analyst_tasks: Verify hash of each collected file matches hash from XDR Search results — confirm integrity
Confirm all targeted file paths are collected before proceeding to E-001
Document collection failure for any path not found — may indicate self-deletion mechanism
expected_outputs: Artifact package collected and available in Response Management
Hash-verified artifact per collected file
escalation_criteria: Collection fails — endpoint agent unreachable despite confirmed isolation status
File path from D-003 not found on endpoint — potential self-deletion mechanism
steps.blocking_condition: C-001 isolation confirmed — this step must complete before E-001
steps.notes: Hard prerequisite for E-001. Do not proceed to file deletion without confirmed artifact collection. If collection fails: retain endpoint in isolation, document failure, and escalate before proceeding

steps.title: Perimeter firewall block for confirmed malicious IPs
steps.objective: Extend network-level IOC blocking to the perimeter beyond endpoint-level Suspicious Object Management coverage
required_inputs: MALICIOUS_IPS finalized from D-004 and T-005
preconditions: C-002 Suspicious Object Management entries created — endpoint-level blocking confirmed
MALICIOUS_IPS list finalized from T-005
analyst_tasks: Export MALICIOUS_IPS list from Suspicious Object Management submission record
Submit to network team via defined channel with incident reference ID and SLA expectation
Track SLA: FIREWALL_TEAM_SLA_HOURS from trigger
Receive and record firewall rule confirmation with rule ID and timestamp
expected_outputs: Firewall rule confirmation from network team with rule ID and activation timestamp
escalation_criteria: FIREWALL_TEAM_SLA_HOURS elapsed without confirmation — escalate to IR Manager
steps.notes: Runs in parallel with Eradication — does not block E-001. Must complete before R-001. Suspicious Object Management (C-002) provides endpoint-level blocking; this step provides perimeter-level blocking. Both are required when MALICIOUS_IPS is non-empty

steps.title: Delete confirmed malicious files from affected endpoints
steps.objective: Remove confirmed malicious payloads from isolated endpoints
required_inputs: Confirmed malicious file paths from D-003 results
preconditions: C-001 isolation confirmed for all target endpoints
C-005 artifact collection confirmed complete for all targeted paths
File paths confirmed malicious from T-005 and T-004 results
IR Lead verbal authorization obtained and documented
tenant_dependencies: Response Management write permission: File Deletion granted to operator
Active endpoint agent on target endpoints
analyst_tasks: Execute delete_file action per confirmed malicious path per endpoint in Response Management
Document IR Lead verbal authorization with timestamp in incident record
Proceed to E-002 verification immediately after deletion
expected_outputs: File deletion confirmed per path per endpoint in Response Management audit log
escalation_criteria: File reappears after deletion — active dropper or persistence mechanism not yet eradicated
Deletion fails on any endpoint — retain isolation and escalate
steps.approval_required: IR Lead verbal authorization — documented, not a formal APR gate
steps.notes: Irreversible. Verify C-005 is complete before executing. Scope deletion strictly to confirmed malicious paths. Do not delete system files or paths that are not confirmed malicious

steps.title: Verify clean state via XDR Search App
steps.objective: Confirm no malicious file indicators remain on affected endpoints after eradication
preconditions: E-001 completed
Q-005 field names validated against tenant telemetry schema
tenant_dependencies: objectHashId field or tenant-equivalent validated — see Query Catalog Q-005
TELEMETRY_FILE event type available in tenant
analyst_tasks: Substitute AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS and MALICIOUS_HASHES into Q-005 and execute
Expected result: zero matches
Export query metadata — query text, execution timestamp, result count = 0 — as clean state audit artifact
If any match found: do not proceed — re-engage E-001 for the matching endpoint
expected_outputs: Zero-result query confirming absence of known malicious indicators on AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS
escalation_criteria: Any match returned by Q-005 — eradication incomplete, persistence mechanism present
steps.notes: Zero-result return is the evidence artifact. Export query metadata (query text, execution timestamp, result count = 0) and retain as EV-017. This evidence is a required input for the R-001 blocking condition

steps.title: Workbench review for residual OAT alerts post-eradication
steps.objective: Confirm no active persistence or secondary payload is generating new alerts on affected endpoints after file deletion
required_inputs: Time window: E-001 completion timestamp through current time
preconditions: E-001 completed
tenant_dependencies: Workbench enabled in tenant
analyst_tasks: Filter Workbench for open OAT alerts or new correlated incidents scoped to AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS after E-001 completion
Confirm no new alerts on affected endpoint scope — document clear status with timestamp
If new alerts present: do not proceed to E-004 — re-engage triage from T-002 for the affected endpoint
expected_outputs: Workbench clear status confirmed — no open OAT alerts on AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS after E-001
escalation_criteria: New OAT alert on any affected endpoint after E-001 — eradication incomplete, persistence not fully removed

steps.title: Patch deployment for CVE-2009-0238 and CVE-2026-32201
steps.objective: Remediate root-cause vulnerabilities on affected endpoints before returning them to production
preconditions: E-001 and E-002 completed — eradication confirmed
E-003 Workbench clear status confirmed
analyst_tasks: Notify patch management team via PATCH_TEAM_CONTACT with CVE list and AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS
Track SLA: PATCH_TEAM_SLA_HOURS from trigger
Receive and record patch deployment confirmation per endpoint before approving R-001
expected_outputs: Patch deployment confirmation per endpoint from patch management system
Patch version and deployment timestamp per endpoint
escalation_criteria: PATCH_TEAM_SLA_HOURS elapsed without confirmation — escalate to IR Manager
Patch unavailable for EOL software — escalate to risk management for compensating control decision
steps.blocking_condition: R-001 is hard-blocked until EH-001 patch confirmation received
steps.notes: Patch deployment is outside Vision One scope. Attack Surface Risk Management can identify remaining unpatched assets estate-wide but does not deploy patches. Engage patch team at start of Containment phase to minimize response timeline

steps.title: Release endpoint isolation
steps.objective: Return clean and patched endpoints to production after all blocking conditions are confirmed
required_inputs: EV-017 Q-005 zero-result verification confirmed for all endpoints
EV-018 patch deployment confirmed for all endpoints
preconditions: E-002 zero-result verification confirmed for all AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS
EH-001 patch deployment confirmation received for all AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS
EH-002 perimeter firewall confirmation received
APR-001 approval from IR Lead received
tenant_dependencies: Response Management write permission: Endpoint Isolation (release action) granted to operator
analyst_tasks: Confirm endpoint status = Active in Response Management after release for each endpoint
Record RELEASE_TIMESTAMP per endpoint for R-002 monitoring window
Set monitoring reminder for MONITORING_WINDOW_HOURS duration
expected_outputs: Endpoint status: Active for all released endpoints
RELEASE_TIMESTAMP recorded per endpoint
escalation_criteria: Release fails for any endpoint — escalate to Trend Micro support
New exploitation indicator appears within MONITORING_WINDOW_HOURS of release
steps.blocking_condition: E-002 clean confirmed AND EH-001 patch confirmed AND EH-002 firewall confirmed AND APR-001 approval received
steps.notes: No exceptions to the blocking condition. Releasing before all conditions are met re-exposes the network to the same CVEs

steps.title: Post-release monitoring via XDR Search App
steps.objective: Detect re-exploitation or persistence reactivation on released endpoints during recovery window
preconditions: R-001 completed
RELEASE_TIMESTAMP set
tenant_dependencies: riskLevel field validated in XDR Search App — see Query Catalog Q-006
Endpoint sensor reporting active on released endpoints
analyst_tasks: Execute Q-006 with AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS and monitoring window — minimum once per analyst shift
Review results for high or critical risk process events
If results found: cross-reference with R-003 Workbench before escalating
expected_outputs: No high or critical risk process events on released endpoints during monitoring window
escalation_criteria: Any high or critical risk event on a released endpoint within monitoring window
steps.notes: Run at minimum once per analyst shift — do not rely on a single execution at window start. Set a recurring task for the full MONITORING_WINDOW_HOURS duration

steps.title: Workbench monitoring for new incident correlation on released endpoints
steps.objective: Detect any new correlated incident involving recently released endpoints during recovery window
preconditions: R-001 completed
tenant_dependencies: Workbench enabled in tenant
analyst_tasks: Filter Workbench for new open incidents involving AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS post-release
Run minimum once per analyst shift for MONITORING_WINDOW_HOURS
If new incident found: re-activate playbook from D-001 with new TIME_WINDOW_START
expected_outputs: No new correlated incidents on released endpoints during monitoring window
escalation_criteria: New correlated incident on any released endpoint within monitoring window

steps.title: Validate Suspicious Object Management entry TTL
steps.objective: Ensure IOC blocks remain active through the full recovery monitoring window
required_inputs: SOL entry list from C-002 evidence EV-011
preconditions: C-002 completed
tenant_dependencies: Suspicious Object Management accessible in tenant
analyst_tasks: Review TTL for all entries created in C-002
Identify entries approaching expiry before end of monitoring window
Renew any entries approaching expiry if monitoring window is still active
Document renewal action if performed — update EV-011
expected_outputs: All Suspicious Object Management entries confirmed active through monitoring window end
escalation_criteria: Any entry expired during monitoring window without renewal — IOC blocking gap created
steps.notes: Set a calendar alert for SOL_TTL_DAYS from C-002 execution to ensure renewal is not missed. TTL expiry creates a re-infection window

steps.title: Backup restore for endpoint with unconfirmable integrity
steps.objective: Restore a verified pre-incident clean state when eradication cannot confirm endpoint integrity
required_inputs: AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS (specific endpoints where integrity is in question)
preconditions: E-002 clean state verification failed for the endpoint OR analyst judgment confirms integrity cannot be established
Backup team availability confirmed
analyst_tasks: Document integrity failure or uncertainty as justification for restore
Notify backup team via BACKUP_TEAM_CONTACT with affected endpoint list and incident reference
Receive restore confirmation with pre-incident backup timestamp
After restore: E-004 patch requirement still applies before R-001 — do not release without patch
expected_outputs: Restore confirmation with verified pre-incident backup timestamp per endpoint
escalation_criteria: Restore required but backup team unavailable — escalate to IR Manager
No clean pre-incident backup available — escalate to risk management for alternative decision
steps.blocking_condition: CONDITIONAL — activate only if E-002 fails or integrity cannot be confirmed. Does not replace E-004 patch requirement
steps.notes: Backup restoration does not substitute for patching. After restore confirmation, E-004 patch requirement still applies before R-001. Vision One does not manage backup infrastructure

steps.title: Detection model review and tuning
steps.objective: Validate and improve OAT detection coverage for T1203 and T1068 based on incident timeline
required_inputs: First exploitation event timestamp from D-003 (EV-003)
First OAT alert timestamp from EV-002
preconditions: Incident closure criteria met or pending final steps
FEATURE_DETECTION_MODEL_MGMT = true
tenant_dependencies: FEATURE_DETECTION_MODEL_MGMT = true
Detection Model Management edit access available to operator — not read-only
analyst_tasks: Compare first exploitation event timestamp (D-003) against first OAT alert timestamp (D-002) to quantify detection lag
Assess whether OAT rules for T1203 and T1068 were enabled, correctly scoped, and appropriately thresholded
Apply tuning adjustments if detection lag indicates threshold or scope issues
Document tuning actions applied or explicitly document that no tuning was required
expected_outputs: Detection gap assessment with quantified lag in minutes
Tuning actions applied or documented as not required
escalation_criteria: Detection lag exceeds 1 hour — exploitation was active well before first alert
OAT rules for T1203 or T1068 were disabled or misconfigured at time of incident
steps.notes: If FEATURE_DETECTION_MODEL_MGMT = false or edit access is read-only: document detection coverage gap and escalate tuning recommendation to tenant administrator

steps.title: Estate-wide unpatched asset scan via Attack Surface Risk Management
steps.objective: Identify remaining endpoints across the full tenant estate not yet patched for CVE-2009-0238 and CVE-2026-32201
preconditions: FEATURE_ASRM = true
tenant_dependencies: FEATURE_ASRM = true
CVE data populated in Attack Surface Risk Management for both CVE identifiers
analyst_tasks: Review asset exposure report for CVE-2009-0238 and CVE-2026-32201 across the full tenant asset estate
Identify endpoints not yet patched beyond original AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS scope
Export report and deliver to patch management team for follow-on estate-wide remediation
external_handoffs: EH-001 for expanded scope beyond AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS
expected_outputs: Unpatched asset report for both CVEs across tenant estate
escalation_criteria: Unpatched asset count exceeds 5% of monitored estate — indicates systemic patching gap
steps.notes: If FEATURE_ASRM = false: document that estate-wide exposure assessment was not performed. Recommend external vulnerability management platform scan as alternative

steps.title: Export Workbench incident record
steps.objective: Produce authoritative incident record for post-incident report and audit retention
preconditions: All required closure criteria confirmed — see closure_criteria section
tenant_dependencies: Workbench export functionality available in tenant
Incident data not yet archived or purged
analyst_tasks: Export full incident record including correlated events, OAT alerts, affected endpoints, and timeline
Verify export completeness — confirm all correlated events from D-001 through R-004 are included
Store export as authoritative incident record for post-incident report and audit
expected_outputs: Complete Workbench incident export file — input for PI-004 and audit
steps.notes: Export must be performed before incident is closed or archived in Workbench. Verify your tenant's data retention policy — if closed incidents are purged, this export is the only persistent audit artifact

steps.title: Post-incident report and IR documentation update
steps.objective: Distribute management report, update IR documentation, and confirm vulnerability tracking
required_inputs: EV-025 Workbench incident export from PI-003
Full evidence package EV-001 through EV-025
EV-023 detection tuning notes from PI-001
EV-024 Attack Surface Risk Management report from PI-002
preconditions: PI-003 Workbench incident export completed
analyst_tasks: Transfer full evidence package to POST_INCIDENT_REPORT_OWNER
Confirm post-incident report distributed to management
Confirm IR documentation and playbooks updated with lessons learned
Confirm vulnerability management team notified for ongoing CVE tracking
expected_outputs: Post-incident report distributed to management
IR documentation updated with lessons from this incident
steps.notes: Governance and compliance requirement — not a technical blocking condition. Set a follow-up reminder per organizational SLA for report distribution confirmation

required: All AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS released from isolation — R-001 confirmed for all
Q-005 zero-result verification confirmed for all AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS — EV-017
EH-001 patch deployment confirmed per endpoint — EV-018
Suspicious Object Management entries active for all confirmed IOCs — R-004 validated
MONITORING_WINDOW_HOURS post-release monitoring completed with no new alerts — R-002 and R-003
conditional: EH-002 perimeter firewall confirmation received — required if MALICIOUS_IPS is non-empty
EH-003 restore confirmation received — required if R-005 was activated
EH-004 identity team reset confirmation received — required if FEATURE_IDENTITY_SECURITY = false
recommended: PI-002 Attack Surface Risk Management report delivered to patch team
PI-001 detection model tuning applied or documented

queries.objective: Identify suspicious child process execution chains from vulnerable application parent processes consistent with CVE-2009-0238 exploitation
queries.baseline_query: processFilePath:"*\\EXCEL.EXE" AND objectFilePath:"*\\Temp\\*" AND eventName:TELEMETRY_PROCESS AND endpointHostName:({{AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS}}) AND eventTime:[{{TIME_WINDOW_START}} TO {{TIME_WINDOW_END}}]

required_field_validation.note: Confirm field contains parent process executable image path in tenant schema
Confirm field contains spawned child process image path
Confirm TELEMETRY_PROCESS is a valid eventName value in this tenant
Confirm field is consistently populated by the deployed sensor type
Confirm time range filter syntax matches tenant Search App version

tenant_substitutions.note: Adapt parent process for CVE-2026-32201 if it targets a different application

telemetry_dependencies: Endpoint sensor (Apex One / Worry-Free / Security Agent) active on AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS
Process creation telemetry ingested for TIME_WINDOW_START through TIME_WINDOW_END
false_positive_notes: Legitimate admin tools and installers may spawn from Temp paths — cross-reference spawned process hash against Threat Intelligence
Correlate with Q-002 outbound connection timing before classifying as confirmed exploitation
queries.expected_result: One or more process execution records with Excel or vulnerable application as parent and unexpected child process or dropped file in Temp path

queries.objective: Identify external C2 or exfiltration connections from affected endpoints following exploitation events
queries.baseline_query: eventName:TELEMETRY_CONNECTION AND endpointHostName:({{AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS}}) AND NOT dst:({{INTERNAL_IP_RANGES}}) AND dpt:(443 OR 8080 OR 4444 OR 80) AND eventTime:[{{TIME_WINDOW_START}} TO {{TIME_WINDOW_END}}]

required_field_validation.note: Confirm TELEMETRY_CONNECTION is a valid eventName value in this tenant
Same as Q-001
Confirm field contains destination IP address string
Confirm field represents destination port number
Confirm time range filter syntax

tenant_substitutions.source: tenant_config variable — must be populated before query use
runtime variable — set to D-003 event timestamp
runtime variable — D-003 event timestamp + 30 minutes recommended

tenant_substitutions.note: Adjust destination ports based on observed attacker infrastructure — baseline is not exhaustive

telemetry_dependencies: Network telemetry source: endpoint sensor network events OR FEATURE_NETWORK_SENSOR
If FEATURE_NETWORK_SENSOR = false: query uses endpoint-reported network telemetry only — coverage may be incomplete for server endpoints
false_positive_notes: Software update services and CDN traffic commonly use port 443 — correlate destination IPs against Threat Intelligence before classifying
Prioritize connections occurring within 5 minutes of Q-001 process events for highest-confidence attribution
queries.expected_result: Outbound connection records to external IPs on common C2 ports within exploitation time window

queries.objective: Detect lateral movement attempts originating from the initially compromised endpoint using remote execution tooling
queries.baseline_query: endpointHostName:"{{AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS[0]}}" AND (objectCmd:"*psexec*" OR objectCmd:"*wmiexec*" OR objectCmd:"*net use*" OR objectCmd:"*wmic*" OR objectCmd:"*schtasks*") AND eventName:TELEMETRY_PROCESS AND eventTime:[{{TIME_WINDOW_START}} TO {{TIME_WINDOW_END}}]

required_field_validation.note: Field name may be commandLine or processCmd in some tenants — validate exact name in this tenant before use
Confirm TELEMETRY_PROCESS is valid
Same as Q-001

tenant_substitutions.source: primary compromised endpoint hostname from runtime variable

tenant_substitutions.note: Extend based on observed attacker TTPs — baseline is not exhaustive

telemetry_dependencies: Endpoint sensor process creation telemetry required
If lateral movement uses network-only channels without process creation (e.g., SMB file copy), this query will not detect it — supplement with Q-002 network data
false_positive_notes: Legitimate administrative use of PsExec, WMIC, and net commands is common in corporate environments — filter by initiating user account and compare against AFFECTED_ACCOUNTS
Correlate with T-003 LSASS access before escalating lateral movement finding
queries.expected_result: Process execution records for lateral movement tooling, or zero-result confirmation of no lateral movement

queries.objective: Detect credential access via LSASS memory read from non-system, non-security processes on affected endpoints
queries.baseline_query: endpointHostName:({{AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS}}) AND objectFilePath:"*\\lsass.exe" AND eventName:TELEMETRY_PROCESS_ACCESS AND eventTime:[{{TIME_WINDOW_START}} TO {{TIME_WINDOW_END}}]

required_field_validation.note: Confirm TELEMETRY_PROCESS_ACCESS is a valid eventName in this tenant — requires process access auditing enabled at sensor level
Confirm field refers to target process path (lsass.exe)
Confirm field contains accessing process path for exclusion filtering
Same as Q-001

telemetry_dependencies: Process access auditing must be enabled at the sensor configuration level
TELEMETRY_PROCESS_ACCESS event type must be present in tenant — validate before relying on this query
false_positive_notes: Security products routinely access LSASS — exclude known agent paths from results
WerFault.exe legitimately accesses LSASS for crash dump generation
Build per-tenant exclusion list from baseline data before using in production
queries.expected_result: LSASS access records from non-system processes, or zero-result confirmation

queries.objective: Confirm no malicious file indicators remain on affected endpoints after eradication actions

required_field_validation.note: Field may be fileHash or objectFileHashSha256 in some tenants — validate exact field name
Confirm TELEMETRY_FILE is a valid eventName for file system events in this tenant
Same as Q-001

tenant_substitutions.source: runtime variable — finalized from T-005 and T-004

telemetry_dependencies: TELEMETRY_FILE event type must be present in tenant telemetry schema
MALICIOUS_HASHES must be populated from T-005 results before executing
false_positive_notes: SHA-256 hash collisions are not a realistic concern — treat all matches as requiring investigation
queries.expected_result: Zero matches — export query metadata (query text, execution timestamp, result count = 0) as EV-017 audit artifact

queries.objective: Detect re-exploitation or persistence reactivation on released endpoints during recovery monitoring window
queries.baseline_query: endpointHostName:({{AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS}}) AND eventName:TELEMETRY_PROCESS AND riskLevel:(high OR critical) AND eventTime:[{{RELEASE_TIMESTAMP}} TO {{MONITORING_WINDOW_END}}]

required_field_validation.note: Confirm riskLevel field is populated by endpoint sensor and searchable in XDR Search App — value set may vary by tenant
Confirm TELEMETRY_PROCESS is valid
Same as Q-001
Confirm time range syntax for post-release window

tenant_substitutions.source: runtime variable — set at R-001 execution

telemetry_dependencies: Endpoint sensor must be active and reporting on released endpoints
riskLevel field population depends on sensor version and configuration — validate
false_positive_notes: High-risk events from security scanners, patch deployments, or administrative scripts are common immediately post-release
Correlate any match against R-003 Workbench review before escalating
queries.expected_result: Zero high or critical risk events during monitoring window — confirms clean recovery

actions.required_license_or_integration: Active endpoint sensor (Apex One / Worry-Free / Security Agent) reporting to this Vision One tenant
preconditions: Endpoint agent online and reachable by Vision One at execution time
Endpoint hostname or agent ID confirmed
APR-001 approval received from IR Lead
irreversible_risks: Severs all user and service network access immediately
Critical service endpoints (domain controllers, database servers) cause service impact on isolation
Not irreversible — reversed by release_isolated_endpoint — but service impact between isolation and release is real
actions.fallback_if_unavailable: Engage network team for VLAN-level isolation via EH-002 if agent is offline or unreachable
actions.notes: Vision One preserves the management channel during isolation — telemetry continues. Isolation does not affect forensic evidence on disk

actions.required_license_or_integration: Active endpoint sensor on target endpoint
preconditions: Target endpoint is isolated — C-001 confirmed
Malicious process PID or image name confirmed from XDR Search results
Process confirmed still running via XDR Search App before execution
C-005 file collection completed if memory forensics may be needed
IR Lead verbal authorization obtained and documented
irreversible_risks: Process termination is immediate and irreversible
Terminating a system process or security agent process may destabilize the endpoint
actions.approval_requirement: IR Lead verbal authorization — documented in incident record, not a formal APR gate
actions.fallback_if_unavailable: Retain endpoint in isolation and proceed to E-001 Delete File without process termination
actions.notes: Confirm process is still running before executing — do not terminate based solely on historical Search results

actions.required_license_or_integration: Active endpoint sensor on target endpoint; file collection feature available in tenant license tier
preconditions: Target endpoint is isolated — C-001 confirmed
File path confirmed from D-003 XDR Search results
File collection quota available in Response Management
actions.fallback_if_unavailable: Use XDR Search result hash as only forensic artifact — document that file collection failed and note as evidence gap
actions.notes: Verify hash of collected file matches hash from XDR Search results to confirm integrity. Hard prerequisite for RA-004 — do not delete without confirmed collection

actions.required_license_or_integration: Active endpoint sensor on target endpoint
preconditions: RA-003 collect_file confirmed complete for all targeted paths
File path confirmed malicious from T-005 and T-004 results
Target endpoint is isolated
IR Lead verbal authorization obtained and documented
irreversible_risks: File deletion is permanent — no undo path
Deleting an incorrect path can damage application or OS components
actions.approval_requirement: IR Lead verbal authorization — documented in incident record, not a formal APR gate
actions.fallback_if_unavailable: Retain endpoint in isolation and escalate — do not release without eradication confirmed
actions.notes: Scope deletion strictly to confirmed malicious file paths. Never delete system files or paths that are not confirmed malicious

preconditions: E-002 zero-result clean state verification confirmed
EH-001 patch deployment confirmed by patch management team
EH-002 perimeter firewall confirmation received
APR-001 approval from IR Lead received
irreversible_risks: Releasing before eradication and patching are confirmed re-exposes the network to the same CVEs
Release action itself is reversible — endpoint can be re-isolated — but re-exposure window creates risk
actions.fallback_if_unavailable: If release fails: escalate to Trend Micro support — do not leave endpoints isolated indefinitely without escalation path
actions.notes: Set RELEASE_TIMESTAMP immediately after successful release for each endpoint — required by Q-006

actions.required_license_or_integration: Vision One with sensor push to endpoint sensor groups enabled
preconditions: IOC confirmed malicious from T-005 Threat Intelligence or T-004 Threat Analysis Center report
IOC type identified: file hash, IP address, domain, or URL
SOL_TTL_DAYS set per organizational policy
irreversible_risks: Entries block matching IOCs estate-wide — not scoped to AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS only
Incorrectly added entries cause widespread blocking across all sensors
actions.approval_requirement: Analyst self-approval — document the evidence basis (T-005 TI result or T-004 detonation report) for each submitted IOC
actions.fallback_if_unavailable: If Suspicious Object Management is unavailable: pass confirmed IOC list to security controls team for manual blocking — classify as external action
actions.notes: Monitor deployment status per sensor group after submission. Entries expire at SOL_TTL_DAYS — set renewal reminder for R-004

actions.required_license_or_integration: Identity Security integration configured — IDENTITY_INTEGRATION_TYPE = azure_ad OR on_prem_ad OR hybrid AND integration status = Connected
preconditions: Compromised account UPN or SAM name confirmed in AFFECTED_ACCOUNTS
APR-001 approval received
Service account impact assessment completed
irreversible_risks: Password reset immediately locks out the account user
Service account reset causes dependent service failures — validate service account status before execution
actions.fallback_if_unavailable: If FEATURE_IDENTITY_SECURITY = false or IDENTITY_INTEGRATION_TYPE = none: activate EH-004 immediately — step classification changes from native to external
actions.notes: For Azure AD accounts: confirm refresh token revocation in addition to password reset. After reset: verify absence of re-authentication via XDR Search using account field

actions.required_license_or_integration: Threat Analysis Center enabled with available submission quota
preconditions: FEATURE_SANDBOX = true
SANDBOX_QUOTA_REMAINING > 0
File artifact available — not already in Threat Intelligence with known verdict
actions.fallback_if_unavailable: If FEATURE_SANDBOX = false or quota exhausted: proceed to T-005 Threat Intelligence lookup only. Document that behavioral analysis was not performed
actions.notes: Detonation occurs in an isolated sandbox environment — no risk to tenant systems. Prioritize unknown file hashes to conserve quota. Review report for: persistence mechanisms, C2 network indicators, dropped secondary payloads, anti-analysis behavior

handoffs.trigger_condition: E-001 and E-002 completed — eradication confirmed. Engage at start of Containment phase to minimize timeline delay
handoffs.expected_acknowledgement: Written patch deployment confirmation per endpoint with patch version and deployment timestamp
handoffs.blocker_impact: R-001 (Release Endpoint) is hard-blocked until this handoff confirmation is received. Endpoints remain isolated indefinitely if SLA is missed
handoffs.evidence_to_retain: EV-018 — patch deployment report per endpoint
handoffs.escalation_path: Escalate to IR Manager if PATCH_TEAM_SLA_HOURS elapsed without confirmation
handoffs.notes: Patch deployment is outside Vision One scope. Attack Surface Risk Management can identify remaining unpatched assets estate-wide but does not deploy patches. EH-001 is also reused in PI-002 for estate-wide remediation beyond AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS

handoffs.trigger_condition: C-002 Suspicious Object Management entries created — confirm MALICIOUS_IPS list is finalized before triggering
required_inputs: MALICIOUS_IPS list from D-004 and T-005 results
handoffs.expected_acknowledgement: Firewall rule reference ID, confirmation of active perimeter block, and timestamp of rule activation
handoffs.blocker_impact: Does not block E-001. Must complete before R-001 is approved. Suspicious Object Management (C-002) provides endpoint-level blocking — perimeter remains open until this handoff completes
handoffs.evidence_to_retain: EV-015 — firewall rule confirmation from network team
handoffs.escalation_path: Escalate to IR Manager if FIREWALL_TEAM_SLA_HOURS elapsed without confirmation
handoffs.notes: Runs in parallel with Eradication phase. Suspicious Object Management and perimeter firewall blocking serve different layers — both are required when MALICIOUS_IPS is non-empty

handoffs.trigger_condition: E-002 clean state verification fails OR analyst judges endpoint integrity cannot be confirmed after eradication — conditional activation only
required_inputs: Affected endpoint names (specific endpoints where integrity is in question)
handoffs.expected_acknowledgement: Restore confirmation with pre-incident backup timestamp and post-restore integrity check result per endpoint
handoffs.blocker_impact: If activated: blocks R-001 for affected endpoints until restore confirmation received. E-004 patch requirement still applies after restore
handoffs.evidence_to_retain: EV-022 — restore confirmation with backup timestamp
handoffs.escalation_path: Escalate to IR Manager if backup team unavailable or no clean backup exists
handoffs.notes: Do not activate preemptively. Backup restoration does not substitute for patching — E-004 patch requirement applies after restore confirmation. Vision One does not manage backup infrastructure

handoffs.trigger_condition: FEATURE_IDENTITY_SECURITY = false OR IDENTITY_INTEGRATION_TYPE = none — replaces RA-007 when Identity Security integration is unavailable
handoffs.expected_acknowledgement: Password reset confirmation per account with timestamp and active session termination confirmation
handoffs.blocker_impact: Compromised credentials remain exploitable until confirmed reset. This handoff effectively blocks E-001 — do not proceed to file deletion while compromised accounts are active
handoffs.evidence_to_retain: EV-013 — identity team acknowledgement and reset timestamp per account
handoffs.escalation_path: Escalate to IR Manager immediately if SLA breaches — active credentials are a containment failure
handoffs.notes: For Azure AD accounts: confirm refresh token revocation in addition to password reset. This is a conditional handoff — only activated when RA-007 is unavailable

handoffs.trigger_condition: PI-003 Workbench incident export completed and all required closure criteria confirmed
required_inputs: EV-025 Workbench incident export from PI-003
Full evidence package EV-001 through EV-024
EV-023 detection tuning notes from PI-001
EV-024 Attack Surface Risk Management report from PI-002
handoffs.expected_acknowledgement: Post-incident report distributed to management and IR documentation update confirmed
handoffs.sla: Tenant-defined — typically 5 to 10 business days post-closure
handoffs.blocker_impact: Does not block incident closure. Governance and compliance requirement
handoffs.notes: Confirm vulnerability management team is notified for ongoing CVE tracking as part of this handoff. IR documentation update should reference playbook version used in this incident

for_C-001: EV-001 Workbench incident record confirming exploitation
EV-003 Q-001 search results confirming exploitation event
Service impact assessment confirming no critical service endpoints in isolation scope without prior owner notification
for_C-004: AFFECTED_ACCOUNTS list with source evidence from T-002 or T-003
Service account impact assessment — confirmation of whether any account is a service account
for_R-001: EV-017 Q-005 zero-result clean state verification for all AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS
EV-018 patch deployment confirmation for all AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS
EV-015 perimeter firewall confirmation from network team
R-004 Suspicious Object Management TTL validation confirmed
Absence of new Workbench alerts on AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS since E-003

approvals.on_timeout: Escalate to APPROVAL_IR_MANAGER — do not auto-approve
approvals.notes: Steps C-001, C-004, and R-001 must never execute without explicit APR-001 approval. Workflow must pause at each gate and wait. Timeout action is escalation only — not automatic approval. All three gates use the same approval_id but are independent gate events — IR Lead must approve each occurrence separately

evidence.artifact_name: OAT alert list with severity and technique mapping
evidence.retention_purpose: Detection model baseline — used in PI-001 to quantify detection lag

evidence.artifact_name: Q-001 exploit child process chain results
evidence.retention_purpose: Exploitation evidence — establishes TIME_WINDOW_START precision and confirms initial compromise vector

evidence.retention_purpose: C2 and exfiltration evidence — source for MALICIOUS_IPS and EH-002 firewall handoff
evidence.export_format: XDR Search App export (CSV or JSON)

evidence.retention_purpose: Asset risk context — documents basis for response prioritization

evidence.retention_purpose: Credential access evidence — required if account compromise escalation is triggered
evidence.export_format: XDR Search App export (CSV or JSON)

evidence.retention_purpose: Behavioral analysis — confirms C2, persistence, and secondary stage capabilities of recovered payload

evidence.retention_purpose: Credential containment record — documents account reset and session termination

evidence.retention_purpose: Forensic preservation — required precondition for E-001 and supports post-incident malware analysis

evidence.artifact_name: Q-005 zero-result clean state verification export
evidence.retention_purpose: Eradication completeness proof — required input for R-001 blocking condition

evidence.retention_purpose: Remediation record — required input for R-001 blocking condition

evidence.retention_purpose: Recovery monitoring record — confirms clean state during monitoring window

evidence.retention_purpose: Authoritative incident record for post-incident report, audit, and IR documentation
